file.content:
# Antiphon

**Status**: design sketch
**Date**: 2026-05-20
**One-line**: an AI moderator that runs multi-party video meetings with the energy of a great D&D DM — prep, opinions, taste, executive floor control — instead of fairly distributing airtime like a librarian.

*Name: Greek "sounding against" — the call-and-response form of structured turn-taking between voices. AI as the cantor leading the antiphony.*

---

## North star

Three frames stack (see ADR 0005):

- **Aesthetic — Brennan Lee Mulligan DM**: opinionated, calls people by name, expects engagement, won't tolerate bullshit. TTRPG actual-play GMing as the comparable craft (Brennan, Matt Mercer, Aabria Iyengar) — pacing, spotlight rotation, scene-cutting, stake-building.
- **Structure — Artemis bridge captain**: the AI coordinates a crew of specialists toward a mission. Captain opens with the brief, tasks specialists by station, surfaces conflicts, synthesizes at decision points, closes cleanly. NOT a chatbot, NOT a turn-taker — the *coordinator* of turns.
- **Energy — A-Team**: tight elite-squad confidence, mutual respect, action-oriented, "I love it when a plan comes together." Speaks like Han Solo, not like HR.

**The AI is on the team, not over the team.** This is the load-bearing anti-pattern: Antiphon is the team's tool, configured by the team, with data that belongs to the team. It is *never* the surveillance product management buys to monitor middle management. If a manager could use Antiphon's outputs to evaluate their reports, the design is wrong shape — and the architecture enforces this, not just the docs (see ADR 0005 §"Hard constraint").

Pitch shape: not "the productivity tool that makes meetings 12% more efficient" but **"the meeting tool that makes meetings not suck."**

---

## The deep move

Stop trying to recreate co-present conversation over video. Co-present TRP timing is sub-200 ms (Stivers et al. 2009) and depends on neural-oscillator entrainment that Zoom destabilizes even at low audio latency (Boland 2022; Seuren 2021). Instead: design a new coordination protocol that uses what the medium is actually good at — parallel typed signals, perfect logging, model-mediated allocation — to do floor management *better than co-present* in some dimensions (fairer, more legible, interruptible-by-design).

**Unifying principle: externalize the social state.** Every piece of floor-management that used to be carried implicitly (gaze, posture, breath, micro-prosody — Kendon 1967, Duncan 1972, Goodwin 1980) becomes a first-class explicit UI object all participants share.

**Frame shift: a video call is a badly-designed multiplayer game.** Import shared HUD overlays, predictive netcode, lag compensation, role indicators, turn timers. We've imported none of these because we kept pretending video chat is a face-to-face meeting. *(This frame is synthesis, not citation — no published work bridges multiplayer-game design to VC turn-taking.)*

---

## Design decisions

### Cut (the co-present-mimicry tax)
- Self-view (Bailenson 2021 — fatigue cause; nobody at a real meeting watches their own face)
- Uniform gallery grid (treats everyone as equally-present every moment)
- Binary mute/unmute (pretends silence/speech are discrete)
- Raise-hand-then-host-calls (classroom power frame; only works because raise-hand is rare)
- "Look at the camera" gaze coaching (asks humans to fake co-presence)
- Sub-200 ms TRP timing as the implicit target (Boland: it's gone; stop chasing)

### Embrace
- **Continuous bid intensity** rendered as a public heat-map (open auction, not sealed-bid)
- **Reason-tagged bids** ("question" / "disagree" / "build-on" / "time-check") feeding allocation — three pieces of info per bidder, all visible
- **Predictive mic warming** — allocate during the current turn, not at the TRP
- **Speaker-aware UI** — current speaker large, on-deck medium, others fade
- **Parallel typed backchannel** with AI-surfaced highlights at handoff
- **Persistent position objects** — pre-meeting takes surface when relevant
- **Soft handoffs with audio ducking** instead of hard turn-boundaries
- **Visible time budgets** per speaker, per topic
- **Mode declaration** at meeting start (brainstorm / decide / inform / socialize) — each loads a different allocation policy
- **Latency legibility** — everyone sees the RTT they're talking through

These compose as decorators around a base voice channel. Stack what the meeting type needs; strip the rest. 1:1 needs almost none; 30-person town hall needs most.

### Executive AI moderator (not advisory, not group self-mod)

The AI *allocates the floor*. Hard executive control. Why:

| | Human moderator | Group self-mod | Executive AI |
|---|---|---|---|
| Signal-tracking capacity | 4-5 (working memory ceiling) | O(N²) distributed | All N in parallel |
| Attention split | Yes (also participating) | Yes (everyone) | No |
| Bias auditability | Vibes-based | Distributed unaccountability | Published policy |
| Cost of cutting senior speakers | High social cost | Nobody does it | Zero |
| Policy drift over meeting | Yes | N/A | Zero |
| Failure mode under latency | "After you / after you" deadlock | Same deadlock | Predictive arbitration |

Operational requirements:
- **~250-400 ms** end-to-end allocation target (~~150-200 ms~~ was wrong — adversarial-panel finding ADR 0004: physically implausible over open internet AND misreads Stivers, which describes turn-end *projection*, not reaction speed). # Roadmap brainstorm — "punt to async" + the anti-pattern that breaks it

**Status**: ideation. Triggered by two connected user directives:

1. *"an important meeting feedback item is 'we're not ready to really discuss this, it needs to be done out of this synchronous context and revisited in a later meeting'"*
2. *"but also things can't always be kicked from meeting to meeting because no one then actually does the work"*

These are the same mechanism with its built-in failure mode. Both have to be in the design or neither belongs.

## The mechanism

A decision currently lands as `open → landed | deferred`. The user's directive adds a third honest landing state: **needs-async** — *"we don't have the right people / data / pre-read for a real sync discussion. Someone owns this between now and the next time we talk about it."*

Concrete type change:

```ts
type DecisionStatus = "open" | "landed" | "deferred" | "needs-async";

interface Decision {
  // existing fields...
  status: DecisionStatus;
  // When status is "needs-async":
  asyncOwner?: ParticipantID;       // who's doing the work between meetings
  asyncDeliverable?: string;        // "draft a one-pager", "get the latency numbers", "talk to legal"
  asyncDueBy?: string;              // a real deadline, not "next meeting"
  // History across meetings — the anti-pattern data:
  puntCount: number;                // how many times this has been punted (deferred OR needs-async OR rolled forward)
  puntHistory: { meetingId: string; status: DecisionStatus; ts: string }[];
}
```

UI: a third button in the Synthesis overlay alongside `LAND IT` / `DEFER`: **`NEEDS ASYNC`**, which opens a small form requiring `asyncOwner` + `asyncDeliverable` + `asyncDueBy` before saving. **The form refuses to save with empty fields** — that's the whole point. "Punted to async" without an owner is the bad state.

## The anti-pattern guard (directive #2)

The simple version of #1 would degrade fast: every uncomfortable decision becomes `needs-async`, no one owns anything, the same item shows up in every recurring meeting, decision-rate goes to zero. Meetings become ritual.

The guard: **the system tracks `puntCount` and surfaces escalation when something has been punted too many times.**

Concrete thresholds:

| puntCount | What the system does |
|---|---|
| 0 | Open decision; nothing special. |
| 1 | Subtle indicator on the decision row: "punted once". |
| 2 | Notice on the host's PROPOSE INTERVENTION panel: *"This has been punted twice — does it need to be re-scoped or assigned?"* |
| 3+ | Captain-class intervention banner when the decision is added to the agenda again: *"This decision has been punted 3 times across 4 meetings. Either someone owns it with a hard deadline today, or we kill it."* (Forces a resolution: assign or close.) |

At `puntCount >= 3`, the synthesis overlay's `NEEDS ASYNC` button **disables** unless the host explicitly overrides (and the override is logged). The system is gently aggressive about not letting the same item slide forever.

## Tracking who actually did the work

When a decision is `needs-async` with an `asyncOwner` and `asyncDueBy`:
- Pre-meeting brief on the next time this decision appears: *"Last meeting, Mei owned 'feasibility numbers' due 2026-05-25. Status?"*
- Decision row in the new meeting shows a small chip: *"Mei · feasibility numbers · due in 2 days"* or *"OVERDUE · 1 day"*
- If the meeting starts and the deliverable is overdue, the captain surfaces this in the opening sequence ("THE BRIDGE" section): *"Mei has an overdue deliverable on 'X'. Address before going further?"*
- If the owner doesn't show up to the next meeting, the system asks the host: *"Mei isn't here — punt this again, reassign, or kill?"*

This is the "no one then actually does the work" check made operational. Every `needs-async` has a person + a thing + a date + a follow-up at the next meeting. None of these is implicit.

## What this rules out

- **Unbounded re-punting.** `puntCount >= 3` requires an explicit decision: assign-with-real-deadline or kill. Not another deferral.
- **Punting without an owner.** The Synthesis form refuses to save `needs-async` without `asyncOwner` and `asyncDeliverable` and `asyncDueBy`.
- **Owner with no deliverable.** Same form refusal. "Mei to think about this" isn't a deliverable.
- **Hand-wavy due dates.** *"Next meeting"* / *"sometime soon"* — UI should require an actual date (date picker, not text input). If the next meeting isn't scheduled, the system suggests a date based on a default (e.g., 5 working days).

## Cross-meeting state — engineering note

This pattern requires persisting decisions across meeting sessions. The current spike's `DEMO_MISSION` is a single in-memory mission. A real implementation needs:
- A `Series` or `Recurring` parent for the meeting series
- Decisions belonging to the series, with `firstSeenAt`, `puntHistory`, `currentOwner`
- The "did the work actually get done?" feedback loop requires participants to update the deliverable status between meetings (could be: link to a doc, status pull from project mgmt tool, just a checkbox the owner toggles)

This is post-v1 architecturally — v1 is single-meeting. But the type model on `Decision` should be drafted now so v1 decisions can later roll up into a series cleanly. **`puntCount` and `puntHistory` can be added to v1's `Decision` even though the spike won't initially use them across meetings.**

## How this lands against ADR 0005's nine principles

| Principle | This pattern's score |
|---|---|
| 1 Shared goals | ✅ Punting an item is an explicit decision *about the goal*, not a side effect |
| 2 Comms | ✅ Punt is a structured channel (status + owner + deliverable + date) not free-text |
| 3 Turns | n/a |
| 4 Tasking specialists | ✅ Async owner IS a specialist task assignment |
| 5 Time focused | ✅ Hard deadline required |
| 6 Respectful | ✅ Naming who owns what publicly creates clarity, not shame; the threshold gate is gentle ("re-scope OR assign", not "X failed") |
| 7 Team focused | ✅ Punting is coop ("we don't have what we need yet"), not adversarial |
| 8 On the team, not over | ⚠️ The "owner overdue" surfacing must go to the team meeting context only, NOT to managers in a separate dashboard. Maintain the hard retention wall. |
| 9 Value-everyone-most-of-the-time | ✅ Punting WHEN appropriate increases value (no one wastes synchronous time on something that needs async work). Anti-pattern guard prevents the value-destroying case. |

## Priority

After Artemis #4 (no-bullshit interventions) and the recurring-meeting series model. The single-meeting spike can add the `needs-async` status and the form requirement now (~50 lines + UI). The `puntCount` escalation and cross-meeting tracking ship with the series model.

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
The user is building "crowdwork" — a system that makes Claude funny in conversations (but only when truly very funny), combining three existing projects: groupchat (ambient meme deployment), slimemold (ambient conversation structure backend), and plancheck (pre-simulation/gap detection). The approach is corpus-based: transcribe YouTube clips, annotate with named structural patterns, eventually build a behavioral contract for Claude. Reference comedians: Jeff Arcuri, Gianmarco Soresi, Eddie Izzard, Norm Macdonald, Brennan Lee Mulligan/Dropout, and Whose Line performers.

In this session: (1) answered the pending commitment_humor design constraint question; (2) ran a large batch transcription session adding 40 new clips; (3) annotated 9 new clip JSON files covering new categories (anti-drop calibration, heckler response, improv game_frame, Game Changer frame_collision); (4) updated schema to v0.3 and CONTENT_LIST.md; (5) began reading remaining unannotated clips (user said "keep going").

2. Key Technical Concepts:
- **commitment_humor deploy conditions for Claude**: flat delivery doesn't transfer to text; announced_premise_collapse is the safe variant; structural variation (same premise, different formats) proves active holding; conversation-bounded; announced_premise_collapse is verbal not tonal intentionality signal
- **anti-drop calibration**: zero anti-drop examples → now 4 documented (Kill Tony ×2, Richards, Chappelle/Musk); Kill Tony format is ideal because working comedian names the failure mechanism on-camera
- **heckler_response as format**: adversarial crowdwork; valid_target rule changes (heckler opted in); authority_reassertion is a distinct move from wit-based reframe; transparent prep reveal defuses "unfair advantage" objection
- **failure_mode taxonomy**: opening_attack, punchline_absent, false_audience_mindread, setup_without_transition, wrong_topic_selected, hostile_setup_primes_rejection, setup:punchline_ratio, valid_target_violation, pattern_collapse_under_pressure, imported_third_party_misfire, reframe_misses_source, excuse_making_on_failure
- **Kill Tony format**: amateur 60-second set → host critique; critique_given field; Tony names structural failures in real time
- **improv game_frame** (Colin Press Conference): dual-reading structure created by game format; simultaneous multi-frame resolution; spontaneous convergence is structurally guaranteed unplanned — resolves the commitment_humor intentionality problem for improv
- **Brennan's frame_collision**: two registers maintained simultaneously with structural bridges (puberty × dark fantasy; wedding × funeral × bar mitzvah × trial × stump speech)
- **Game Changer format**: game_show competitive improv; comedians face absurd prompts; Brennan's theatrical escalation instinct conflicts with simple game answers
- **faster-whisper**: small model, CPU, int8; sequential batching required (parallel crashes from memory exhaustion)
- **valid_target rule**: situation/self/shared_frustration/abstract = allowed; identity/unchosen = never; heckler = special case (opted in by disrupting)

3. Files and Code Sections:

- `$HOME/Documents/crowdwork/corpus/schema.json` (updated to v0.3)
  - Added format_types: "heckler_response", "improv (game)", "game_show (competitive improv)", "stand-up (open mic + host critique)"
  - Added reframe variant: "heckler_reframe"
  - Added annotation fields: commitment_type (for commitment_humor), failure_mode (for anti_drop), critique_given boolean (for Kill Tony-style clips)

- `$HOME/Documents/crowdwork/corpus/CONTENT_LIST.md`
  - Added Brennan (2 annotated + [~] 15 transcribed), Colin (1 annotated + [~] 8 transcribed), Troy Bond (2 annotated + [~] 7 transcribed), Kill Tony ×2, Richards, Chappelle/Musk, Soresi Furry Convention
  - Added "Heckler response" section

- `$HOME/Documents/crowdwork/corpus/clips/anti-drop-killtony-1.json` (NEW)
  - Source: Kill Tony 84UoEjnIHWI — 3 acts
  - Act 1 (Psy): failure_modes: opening_attack + distraction + punchline_absent + false_audience_mindread. Tony's critique names all four explicitly.
  - Act 2 (Juanita): setup_without_transition; accidental self-proving honest_observation ("painfully shy, taking forever to get to the point" = describes herself)
  - Act 3 (Mike Thielacker): wrong_topic_selected; interesting life (Black Hawk crew chief, medically retired, ex-wife) vs. chosen material (fart story)

- `$HOME/Documents/crowdwork/corpus/clips/anti-drop-killtony-2.json` (NEW)
  - Source: Kill Tony PS3kLyyMI0Y — 3 acts
  - Act 1 (Wayne McCracken): excuse_making_on_failure; "standing ovation" wordplay technically correct but flat-lined; Tony shuts down each excuse
  - Act 2 (Keenan McKenna): hostile_setup_primes_rejection; deaf school joke structurally sound but "I love gun violence" setup made audience hostile before punchline; Tony: "I thought you were great" — separates craft from room response
  - Act 3 (Stephanie Robertson): setup:punchline_ratio + wrong_topic_selected; Tony's workshop names exact edits; "shorter is better" principle; "stay with me" as room-hostility signal; Tony offers to deliver punchline himself

- `$HOME/Documents/crowdwork/corpus/clips/anti-drop-richards.json` (NEW)
  - Source: amjUNF_R_PY — Laugh Factory 2006
  - failure_mode: valid_target_violation + pattern_collapse_under_pressure
  - Wit-based reframe collapsed under pressure → identity attack substituted
  - Heckler wins: "never had a show, never had a movie" is clean honest_observation on Richards' career; Richards capitulates

- `$HOME/Documents/crowdwork/corpus/clips/anti-drop-chappelle-musk.json` (NEW)
  - Source: BdBga225HBk — Chase Center Dec 2022
  - failure_mode: imported_third_party_misfire + reframe_misses_source
  - Three recovery attempts all address wrong source of hostility: (1) "people you fired" — wrong source; (2) "terrible seats" — most absurd; (3) "oh fuck it" — correct abort, too late
  - Rule: when room is hostile about the imported third party, only (a) name the actual hostility or (b) immediate abort works; reframing extends/deepens the breach

- `$HOME/Documents/crowdwork/corpus/clips/troy-bond-spider-man.json` (NEW)
  - Source: 18drJ1fdANc
  - format: heckler_response; variant: heckler_reframe
  - Heckler's own prop (Spider-Man shirt) used as vehicle for condemnation; "where great power comes great responsibility / where pink blazer comes annoying cunt"
  - "I learned that from Uncle Ben" — fictional third party recruited from heckler's own shirt
  - "Don't try to jump in, Robin" — pre-empts friend; assigns subordinate role using same universe
  - "Don't ever attack a nerd" — self_aware_failure weaponized: owns the nerd identity

- `$HOME/Documents/crowdwork/corpus/clips/troy-bond-incredibles.json` (NEW)
  - Source: 2gAPKVEx9zg
  - format: heckler_response; variant: authority_reassertion
  - "I got like a hundred of them ready to go" — transparent prep reveal defuses objection preemptively
  - "Me too. You're killing it." — apparent_validation redefining "killing it"
  - "You think I got into comedy for overwhelming majority opinion? No!" — authority_reassertion: comedian redefines social contract of performance; stage is not democratic
  - Note: authority_reassertion only works after comedian has already won the exchange

- `$HOME/Documents/crowdwork/corpus/clips/colin-press-conference.json` (NEW)
  - Source: ARoCpelSF70 — Whose Line Press Conference game
  - variant: game_frame; pattern: callback_window
  - Game creates dual-reading structure: every question works as Batman AND coming-out question simultaneously
  - "I'm not sure but I may want to rephrase that later" → "you might want to rephrase that now" — live self_aware_failure
  - Final answer: "Batman who's declaring that he's coming out of the closet" resolves both frames simultaneously
  - Key insight: improv format guarantees spontaneous convergence is unplanned → solves commitment_humor intentionality problem for improv context

- `$HOME/Documents/crowdwork/corpus/clips/soresi-furry-convention.json` (NEW)
  - Source: wa4syN0bBbw — Furpocalypse convention set, ~25 min
  - 5 annotated entries:
  - (1) solidarity_reframe opener: theater kid / furry community
  - (2) fursona vote game_frame (game_frame running full 25 min; "Don't say it yet")
  - (3) "we're on the same train" — Nazi/Jewish solidarity_reframe with Holocaust resonance
  - (4) Beanie baby ominous poem: "I'll be there with you at the end" — precise_property_isolation (truncation + ominousness simultaneous)
  - (5) IT furries → planes → ISIS logical_escalation chain

- `$HOME/Documents/crowdwork/corpus/clips/brennan-birds-bees.json` (NEW)
  - Source: zrSnMoLXfjc — Game Changer short
  - format: game_show (competitive improv); pattern: frame_collision
  - commitment_type: dual_frame_maintenance
  - Puberty talk × dark fantasy; structural bridges preserved (have you touched yourself? → not with your hand; birds build nests → dark sun built a sword)
  - "Have you opened the eye within?" — sexual metaphor completely replaced by epic quest element
  - Never breaks either frame; humor lives in the mapping between registers

- `$HOME/Documents/crowdwork/corpus/clips/brennan-wedding-speech.json` (NEW)
  - Source: ADe25S45hxk — Game Changer short
  - format: game_show (competitive improv); pattern: frame_collision
  - commitment_type: accumulating_frames
  - Occasions accumulate: wedding → eulogy → bar mitzvah → court trial → stump speech
  - Speech format is the constant; contexts accumulate incompatibly
  - Compare to Norm OJ: Norm's premise is constant across varying contexts; here contexts accumulate while premise (speech format) is constant

4. Errors and Fixes:
- **Memory crash from 17 parallel transcription processes**: User said "17 is too many in parallel and we ran out of memory and crashed." Fixed by using sequential bash loops (one transcription at a time per loop), running two sequential loops simultaneously maximum. The error was running `python transcribe.py` 16 times in parallel background processes simultaneously — each loads faster-whisper model into RAM (~850MB each), causing OOM.
- **wa4syN0bBbw included in Brennan's Shorts loop**: The Soresi Furry Convention URL was included in the Brennan Shorts batch loop (using wrong URL format `shorts/wa4syN0bBbw`). Also started separately as a proper URL. Both ran, one overwrote the other — result was correct since same model/audio.

5. Problem Solving:
- Commitment_humor intentionality problem: resolved with three-part framework (announced entry, structural variation, defined endpoint). Announced_premise_collapse is safe path; sustained-weeks variant not deployable for Claude.
- Anti-drop calibration: went from zero examples to 4 documented failure cases. Kill Tony identified as purpose-built format for this. failure_mode taxonomy established from Tony's real-time critiques.
- Heckler response patterns: Troy Bond establishes heckler_reframe + authority_reassertion as distinct patterns. Valid_target rule updated: heckler has opted in. Transparent prep reveal ("100 ready to go") is a distinct tactic.
- Improv intentionality problem: Colin Press Conference resolves it — game format structurally guarantees spontaneous convergence is unplanned, so the audience knows the comedian found it rather than planted it.

6. All user messages:
